title: Condom ad during India vs England T20 match sparks debate: World Cup winner says, ‘Children are watching’
content:
• Kirti Azad, who was a part of India's 1983 World Cup squad, is a TMC MLA.
• Condoms are included in the Class 10 NCERT syllabus.
• The BCCI passes restrictions down to streaming platforms, while broadcasters independently control day-to-day commercial decisions.

title: Tragedy strikes Pune’s Agarwal family again as Ketan's grandfather passes away
content:
• Devichand Agarwal, 71, founder of Pune's Success Group, passed away due to cardiac arrest, reportedly from distress after his grandson's death.
• His grandson, 26-year-old Ketan Agarwal, a Director at the family firm, died on June 18 after falling from Lohagad Fort near Pune.
• Ketan Agarwal's death was allegedly a murder orchestrated by his 20-year-old fiancée Siya Goyal and her lover Chetan Choudhary, making his fall appear accidental.

title: 'Death threats' to PM Modi ahead of Melbourne visit, says report; Australian police probe
content:
• Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to visit Melbourne, Australia, which includes participation in the 'Melbourne Meets Modi' community event.
• The 'Melbourne Meets Modi' community event is scheduled to be held at Marvel Stadium on 9 July.
• The Australian Federal Police are investigating death threats reportedly issued on social media against PM Modi ahead of his visit.

title: Suvendu Adhikari announces Rs 313 crore Pradhan Mantri Cha Shramik Protsahan Yojana for West Bengal tea workers
content:
• West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced the Pradhan Mantri Cha Shramik Protsahan Yojana (PMCSPY) with a total financial allocation of Rs 313.30 crore for tea garden workers in North Bengal.
• Under the PMCSPY, Rs 177 crore is allocated for the Cha Shramik Shiksha Yojana (CSSY), Rs 72 crore for the Cha Shramik Swasthya Suraksha Yojana (CSSSY), and Rs 63 crore for the Cha Shramik Aashray Yojana (CSAY) to construct 321 resting sheds.
• The North Bengal Development Department (NBDD) will serve as the nodal implementing agency for the Pradhan Mantri Cha Shramik Protsahan Yojana (PMCSPY).

title: India-Israel investment pact comes into effect
content:
• The India-Israel investment pact, signed in September 2025, allows investors at least three years to exhaust domestic legal remedies for dispute settlement, a relaxation from India's 2016 model treaty's five-year period.
• This is India's first bilateral investment treaty with an Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) member since adopting its new model treaty text in 2016, replacing the 1996 agreement terminated in 2017.
• Between April 2000 and March 2026, India received $371 million in foreign direct investment from Israel, accounting for 0.05% of total FDI inflows during the period.

title: Finance Ministry notifies rules for determination of origin of goods under India-UK trade pact
content:
• The Finance Ministry has notified the 'Customs Tariff (Determination of Origin of Goods under Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement between India and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland) Rules, 2026', which will come into force on July 15, 2026.
• The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) is expected to provide free access for 99 per cent of India's exports to the UK.
• Bilateral commerce between India and the UK grew 8.62 per cent to USD 25.12 billion in 2025-26, with India reporting a trade surplus of USD 1.76 billion in the last fiscal year.

title: Enrol under PM Fasal Bima Yojana by July 31: Maharashtra agriculture minister to farmers
content:
• The enrolment deadline for the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) is July 31.
• Under PMFBY, farmers pay a premium of 2% of the sum insured for foodgrains, pulses, and oilseeds, and 5% for commercial crops such as cotton and onion.
• To be eligible for PMFBY, farmers must have at least 0.10 hectare under the notified crop, and submitting false information will lead to debarment from Agriculture Department schemes for five years.

title: India-Israel bilateral investment pact comes into force
content:
• The Bilateral Investment Agreement (BIA) between India and Israel was signed on 8th September 2025 in New Delhi.
• The Bilateral Investment Agreement (BIA) between India and Israel came into force on 04 July 2026.
• India and Israel signed the Terms of Reference for negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) in November last year, as announced by Union Minister of Commerce & Industry Piyush Goyal.

title: India waives customs duty, IGST on imported animals under UK trade pact from July 15
content:
• India will waive customs duty and IGST on imported animals under a UK trade pact, effective from July 15, as per a notification issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC).
• Importers of these animals are required to execute a bond equal to the value of the imported animals and furnish a bank guarantee or cash deposit equivalent to 110% of the customs duty otherwise payable.
• Imported animals must be re-exported within six months from the date of customs clearance, though for government entities and diplomatic missions, the Commissioner of Customs can extend this by six months at a time, up to a maximum of two years.

title: Most Indians seek protein-rich snacks; 61% favour natural sweeteners: Report
content:
• Nearly 86% of respondents consider protein an important factor when choosing snacks, with 32% willing to pay a premium for protein-rich products.
• 61% of consumers prefer snacks sweetened with natural ingredients like jaggery or dates over refined sugar.
• Ingredient transparency is considered the most important factor by 62% of respondents when choosing a snack brand.
